sections.question: Whether a committee which is required to file an abbreviated statement in connection with an election is also required to file a semi-annual statement for the six-month period during which the election has been held.
sections.conclusion: The Commission has rejected the reading that Section 84206 can be satisfied by the filing of abbreviated statements. While one could read the last clause of Section 84206 to apply to abbreviated statements and exempt those who file abbreviated statements, to do so would sacrifice too much of the statutory scheme. The Commission's interpretation harmonizes both sections of the Act. The last paragraph of Section 84204.1, which requires the information contained in abbreviated statements to be repeated in subsequent statements, indicates that abbreviated statements are not to substitute for others required under the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a committee file both an abbreviated statement for an election and a semi-annual statement covering the same six-month period?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, both filings are required. The abbreviated statement and semi-annual statement serve different purposes under the Political Reform Act and cannot substitute for one another. The statutory language and legislative scheme demonstrate that abbreviated statements must be supplemented by the semi-annual statements required under Section 84206.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is marked as 'Opinion Request No. 80-002' but FPPC states 'no formal opinion will be issued' due to the question not raising a novel interpretation issue. Response addresses the question substantively despite declining to issue formal opinion. Document ID 80A149 appears to be archival reference number; Opinion Request No. 80-002 is the official case number.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a committee which is required to file an abbreviated statement in connection with an election is also required to file a semi-annual statement for the six-month period during which the election has been held.

CONCLUSION: The Commission has rejected the reading that Section 84206 can be satisfied by the filing of abbreviated statements. While one could read the last clause of Section 84206 to apply to abbreviated statements and exempt those who file abbreviated statements, to do so would sacrifice too much of the statutory scheme. The Commission's interpretation harmonizes both sections of the Act. The last paragraph of Section 84204.1, which requires the information contained in abbreviated statements to be repeated in subsequent statements, indicates that abbreviated statements are not to substitute for others required under the Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC addressed whether election committees must file both abbreviated statements and semi-annual statements for overlapping six-month periods. The Commission confirmed that both filings are required because they serve different reporting purposes and abbreviated statements cannot substitute for comprehensive semi-annual reporting.
